actions.value:
Regarding the “Authorize Device” warning:

This is a security measure that's enabled by default and triggered when our servers detect a login from a new or un-permissioned device. If an unauthorized person obtained or guessed your login credentials, this prevents them from quickly logging in and accessing your funds without your permission.

 

 

For immediate access to your Edge account, you can:

  • Use the 2FA code (if you backed it up before this).
  • Use a logged-in device to permission a new device.
  • Wait the indicated number of days for an unrecognized device to become permissioned.

 

If your account has been dormant for awhile, that last option will be increasingly long. If you can authenticate yourself by providing the following information, we can reduce this lock-out period to its minimum of 7 days.

  1. Username of the account
  2. Date and location you recently attempted to log in and received this IP lock-out
  3. Device type or model you are attempting to login with
  4. City or region that you historically access the account from
  5. Approximately when you were last logged into your account
  6. The year the account was created

 

After the waiting period has elapsed, you may login with your username and password to gain full access to your account. Once you're back in, you may go into Menu (≡) > Settings > Account Settings to make a record of your 2-Factor Security / Backup Code and be able to rapidly permission a new device, if you're ever in this situation again.

 

Please review our support article for more information: https://support.edge.app/hc/en-us/articles/7018106439579-Edge-Security-IP-Validation-and-2FA
